question: Synthesise Finnveden, Riedel, and Shulman's claims about lock-in feasibility with Davidson and Houlden's claims about software intelligence explosion compression to evaluate the size of the decision window humanity has to set up institutions before lock-in becomes possible. How tight is that window if the SIE compression scenario plays out?

expected_citations.title: How quick and big would a software intelligence explosion be?

required_elements: AGI and Lock-in argues at least 20% subjective probability that a trillion-year stable society is feasible
Lock-in becomes plausible once AGI can preserve goals via stored minds/specifications and prevent drift via surveillance
The SIE compression paper estimates ~60% probability of compressing 3+ years of AI progress into <1 year
Combining: the decision-making window for institutional choices about lock-in compresses from years to months under the central SIE scenario
Implication: pre-AGI agreements / institutional design must be in place before the compression begins, not negotiated during
